title: Plan console entry-point consolidation
goal: Inventory Bureau console scripts and systemd references, then design a compatibility-preserving subcommand consolidation path.

metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #235: added a compatibility-preserving console entry-point consolidation plan, a bounded entry-point inventory report, and tests covering packaged console scripts, module entry points, systemd ExecStart references, and plan completeness.
metadata.coordination_note: Bureau PR #234 was merged before this closeout because it was green/mergeable and touched registry/queue.json; closeout is based on Bureau main after #234.
validation: Bureau PR #235 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local before merge: entrypoint/systemd focused tests -> 28 passed.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local before merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m ruff check src tests -> pass.
Post-merge closeout base: entrypoint/systemd focused tests -> 28 passed.
Post-merge closeout base: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.

verified_acceptance.entrypoint-inventory: Inventory lists 15 packaged console scripts from pyproject.toml and records module targets, layers and documentation reference files.
verified_acceptance.systemd-impact: Inventory records 6 systemd service ExecStart commands and timers; plan reviews systemd impact for bureau, source PR bridge, review steward, agent frontier and Codex bridge units.
verified_acceptance.compat-plan: Plan forbids removals now, adds future bureau grouped aliases first, keeps compatibility shims before any removal, forbids default warnings in JSON/scheduler paths, and defines a removal gate.

acceptance.assertion: All console entry points and their module targets are listed.
Known systemd units, docs and scripts that call the entry points are checked.
Plan keeps compatibility shims before any removal and states when warnings or removal are allowed.
